text: Class imbalance is one of the longstanding and significant challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite significant advances over several decades, the class imbalance problem still remains a major impediment to many applications of both machine learning and data mining techniques. State-of-the-art research in the area has been advancing the field by several folds in terms of understanding the class imbalance problem and its related challenges. This field is rapidly evolving, hence this article provides a summary of some of the recent research contributions and state-of-the-art methods proposed for tackling the problem of class imbalance, and acts as a survey of various techniques for balancing class distribution, specifically focusing on classification and also touching upon regression, as well as deep long-tail learning, while depicting the practical impact of these solutions in various fields such as management science and engineering, and providing an outlook of the emerging challenges in this area.
